topics.title: Aero Discovery overview
topics.summary: Aero Discovery converts property-operating responses into an explainable readiness profile, risk view and governed pilot roadmap.
details: Adaptive questions change with prior answers.
Users can review and revise decisions.
Outputs support executive and implementation conversations.

topics.title: Assessment method
topics.summary: The assessment captures business context, workflows, systems, data readiness, risks, delivery preferences, stakeholders and budget.
details: Conditional questions reduce irrelevant capture.
Inline feedback explains implications.
Review mode supports reversible decisions.

topics.title: Data governance foundation
topics.summary: Advanced automation should follow agreement on authoritative sources, entity relationships, ownership, quality rules, privacy classification and the reporting layer.
details: Assign business owners and operational data stewards.
Define validation, reconciliation and exception rules.
Publish a controlled data dictionary and source map.

topics.title: Property entity relationship model
topics.summary: The governed model links properties to units, leases to units and tenant parties, payments to lease obligations, maintenance cases to assets and contractors, and documents to governed entities.
details: Use stable identifiers across systems.
Preserve history rather than overwriting time-bound relationships.
Define referential integrity and duplicate-resolution rules.

topics.title: Authoritative source design
topics.summary: Each entity and metric requires an agreed authoritative source, extraction method, owner, refresh frequency and reconciliation control.
details: Accounting normally governs posted financial transactions.
Property management normally governs property, unit and lease operations.
CRM governs relationship and opportunity records; document storage governs controlled files.

topics.title: Reporting and semantic layer
topics.summary: A governed reporting layer should stage, validate and reconcile source data before publishing certified measures and dashboards.
details: Define measure formula, grain, owner and refresh cadence.
Separate data preparation from presentation.
Prevent competing spreadsheet definitions of executive metrics.

topics.title: Current-state workflow mapping
topics.summary: Current-state maps should document triggers, actors, systems, decisions, controls, hand-offs, service times, evidence and exception paths.
details: Validate maps through stakeholder walkthroughs.
Record manual rework and duplicate capture.
Identify policy, technology and ownership constraints.

topics.title: Future-state workflow design
topics.summary: Future-state workflows should remove avoidable friction while preserving control, accountability, escalation, exception handling and measurable service outcomes.
details: Define system boundaries and approval gates.
Set service levels and customer communications.
Include rollback and human override.

topics.title: Service levels and exception paths
topics.summary: Every operational service should define request channels, triage, priority, ownership, acknowledgement and resolution targets, escalation and closure evidence.
details: Use business calendars and severity definitions.
Measure elapsed time and outcome quality.
Review breached and repeatedly reopened cases.

topics.title: Stakeholder interface model
topics.summary: The operating model requires role-specific interfaces for owners, managers, tenants, contractors, estate agents and executives.
details: Apply least-privilege access.
Expose role-relevant decisions and evidence.
Define hand-offs between portals, WhatsApp and service teams.

topics.title: Existing systems integration baseline
topics.summary: Existing accounting, property management, CRM, BI, cloud storage, spreadsheet, listing and payment capabilities provide the practical starting point for integration.
details: Improve identifiers and export controls first.
Reconcile before automating.
Introduce APIs and connectors where they reduce measured risk or effort.

topics.title: Hybrid delivery approach
topics.summary: The hybrid approach reduces delivery risk by improving current tools and controls before commissioning a custom platform.
details: Stabilise data and workflows first.
Prove value with bounded pilots.
Use custom development only where product differentiation or scale requires it.

topics.title: Integrated PropTech operating model
topics.summary: The intended model extends beyond isolated automation toward an integrated PropTech operating capability with governed services, platforms, data, vendors and support.
details: Define platform and service ownership.
Establish support tiers and vendor accountability.
Create a continuous-improvement cadence.

topics.title: Pilot scope
topics.summary: A strong pilot is bounded, measurable and focused on a small number of workflows, datasets, interfaces and user groups.
details: Define acceptance criteria and KPIs.
Limit interfaces to those needed to prove value.
Confirm owners, controls and escalation paths.

topics.title: Project 1 - Property Operations Data Baseline
topics.summary: Create a governed dataset covering properties, units, tenants, leases, payments, arrears, vacancies, maintenance and key documents.
details: Deliver a data dictionary and source map.
Assign ownership and privacy classification.
Publish quality and reconciliation results.

topics.title: Project 2 - Executive Performance Dashboard
topics.summary: Build an executive dashboard for occupancy, collections, arrears, vacancy days, maintenance cost, response time and portfolio performance.
details: Publish metric definitions and the data model.
Document refresh and access processes.
Provide management interpretation and screenshots.

topics.title: Project 3 - Rental or Maintenance Workflow Automation
topics.summary: Automate one low-risk workflow with approvals, exception handling, audit evidence, user testing and measured time saving.
details: Map the process before automating.
Retain human override.
Measure baseline and post-pilot performance.

topics.title: Project 4 - PropTech Pilot Operating Model
topics.summary: Design the target operating model connecting existing systems, stakeholder portals, WhatsApp, reporting and governance.
details: Produce architecture and integration views.
Define RACI and service catalogue.
Document costs, risks and pilot mobilisation.

topics.title: Project 5 - AI-Assisted Operations Proof of Concept
topics.summary: Prototype a governed assistant for tenant enquiries, document classification or management reporting using approved data only.
details: Define the use case and data boundary.
Test prompts and responses.
Require human approval and complete a risk assessment.

topics.title: Automation controls and exceptions
topics.summary: Automation should use explicit approvals, audit logs, retry limits, exception queues, human override and controlled rollback.
details: Do not automate unstable or undefined processes.
Make duplicate execution safe.
Assign exception ownership and response targets.

topics.title: Governed AI assistant controls
topics.summary: A governed assistant should operate only on approved data, identify its evidence boundary, avoid autonomous consequential decisions and escalate uncertainty to humans.
details: Log prompts and outputs appropriately.
Protect personal and financial data.
Test unsupported, adversarial and ambiguous requests.

topics.title: Executive property performance metrics
topics.summary: Core metrics include occupancy, collection rate, arrears value and ageing, vacancy days, maintenance cost, response and resolution time, repeat incidents and portfolio yield.
details: Define formula and grain.
Assign an accountable metric owner.
Reconcile to authoritative records.

topics.title: Commercial model and business outcomes
topics.summary: The discovery profile includes a pilot budget and multiple revenue models so delivery can be tied directly to measurable business outcomes.
details: Consider subscriptions, managed services, transaction fees, listing income, commissions and white-label licensing.
Separate implementation from recurring service costs.
Use acceptance criteria for milestone payments.

topics.title: Stakeholder governance and change management
topics.summary: Disciplined reviews, decision logs, change communication, adoption measures and accountable owners are required for sustainable transformation.
details: Record rationale and approvers.
Assess role and process impacts.
Track adoption and resistance signals.

topics.title: Sanitized evidence and applied capability
topics.summary: Publish sanitized process maps, dashboards, prototypes, decision records and outcome reports that demonstrate applied capability without exposing sensitive data.
details: Remove personal and financial identifiers.
Retain enough structure to prove the method.
Record assumptions and limitations.

topics.title: Capability development path
topics.summary: Required capabilities span process mapping, service design, data literacy, cloud and security basics, stakeholder analysis, Power Query, Excel controls, Power BI, connectors, automation and governance.
details: Apply skills directly to the five projects.
Use peer and stakeholder reviews.
Capture evidence of decisions and outcomes.

topics.title: Target architecture
topics.summary: The recommended architecture reuses existing systems, establishes governed identifiers and integration contracts, adds a reporting layer and introduces automation in phases.
details: Use server-side identity and secrets.
Log privileged and integration activity.
Separate browser demonstration storage from production persistence.

topics.title: Governance and risk
topics.summary: Each material risk should map to an owner, control, evidence requirement, escalation path and review cadence.
details: Prioritise access control and auditability.
Include service continuity and reconciliation.
Validate third-party and integration responsibilities.

topics.title: POPIA and privacy
topics.summary: Production delivery should apply purpose limitation, minimum necessary data, lawful processing, retention rules and data-subject workflows.
details: Do not expose secrets in the browser.
Record consent and communication preferences where required.
Define breach and access-request procedures.

topics.title: WhatsApp integration
topics.summary: WhatsApp should be a governed interaction channel rather than the source of truth, with verified server-side webhooks and explicit human escalation.
details: Govern templates and opt-ins.
Map conversations to governed entities.
Store credentials and signatures server-side.

topics.title: Accessibility and responsive UX
topics.summary: The portal should support mobile and desktop layouts, keyboard interaction, reduced motion, theme adaptation and clear non-visual status communication.
details: Use semantic labels and focus management.
Do not rely on colour alone.
Keep touch targets usable on mobile.

topics.title: Aero reactive 3D agent
topics.summary: Aero uses a lightweight Three.js/WebGL character with pointer tracking and state-driven gestures across landing, wizard and chat placements.
details: States include idle, welcome, thinking, explaining, alert, working and success.
Rendering pauses off-screen.
Reduced-motion and fallback behavior remain available.

topics.title: Repository implementation and testing
topics.summary: The application is a static GitHub Pages demonstration with modular assets, knowledge, CI validation, Lighthouse audits and browser-level functional testing.
details: Validate critical journeys on mobile and desktop.
Check JavaScript and JSON syntax.
Production AI requires a secure webhook backend.

topics.title: Recommended next steps
topics.summary: Validate stakeholders and workflows, approve authoritative sources and KPI definitions, assign owners and controls, then mobilise the five projects through a governed pilot backlog.
details: Confirm data and system owners.
Map risks to controls.
Approve architecture, budget and support model.
